calendar.name: create_event
calendar.description: Creates a calendar event. Use this tool for queries like: * Create an event on my calendar for tomorrow at 2pm called 'Meeting with Jane'. * Schedule a meeting with john.doe@google.com next Monday from 10am to 11am. Example: create_event( summary='Meeting with Jane', startTime='2024-09-17T14:00:00', endTime='2024-09-17T15:00:00' ) # Creates an event on the primary calendar for September 17, 2024 from 2pm to 3pm called 'Meeting with Jane'.

Attachment.description: A file attachment for an event.

fileUrl.description: Required. URL link to the attachment.

additionalGuests.description: Optional. Number of additional guests. Optional. The default is 0.

comment.description: Output only. The attendee's response comment. Optional. Read-only.

email.description: Required. The attendee's email address. This field must be present when adding an attendee. It must be a valid email address as per RFC5322.

id.description: Output only. The attendee's Profile ID. Optional. Read-only.

optionalAttendee.description: Optional. Whether this is an optional attendee. Optional. The default is False.

organizer.description: Output only. Whether the attendee is the organizer of the event. Read-only. The default is False.

resource.description: Optional. Whether the attendee is a resource. Can only be set when the attendee is added to the event for the first time. Subsequent modifications are ignored. Optional. The default is False.

responseStatus.description: Optional. The attendee's response status. Possible values are: * `needsAction` - The attendee has not responded to the invitation (recommended for new events). * `declined` - The attendee has declined the invitation. * `tentative` - The attendee has tentatively accepted the invitation. * `accepted` - The attendee has accepted the invitation.

self.description: Output only. Whether this entry represents the calendar on which this copy of the event appears. Read-only. The default is False.

guestsCanInviteOthers.description: Optional. Whether attendees other than the organizer can invite others to the event. Optional.

guestsCanModify.description: Optional. Whether attendees other than the organizer can modify the event. Optional.

guestsCanSeeGuests.description: Optional. Whether attendees other than the organizer can see who the event's attendees are. Optional.

method.description: Required. How the reminder is delivered to the user. Possible values are: * `email` - Reminders are sent via email. * `popup` - Reminders are sent via a UI popup.

minutes.description: Required. Number of minutes in advance that the reminder should be delivered.

customLocationLabel.description: Optional. The label for a custom location. Required if type is CUSTOM_LOCATION.

type.description: Optional. The type of working location. Optional.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Unspecified working location type. Will be treated as HOME_OFFICE.
Home office.
Custom location.

addGoogleMeetUrl.description: Optional. Whether to create and attach a video conference URL for the event. Defaults to False.

allDay.description: Optional. Whether the event is an all-day event. The default is False. If true, the start and end time are treated as midnight.

attachments.description: Optional. File attachments for the event. Optional.

attendeeEmails.description: Optional. Deprecated: use `attendees` instead. The additional attendees of the event, as email addresses.

attendees.description: Optional. The additional attendees of the event.

availability.description: Optional. Whether the event blocks time on the calendar. Optional.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. Will be treated as BUSY.
The event blocks time on the calendar.
The event does not block time on the calendar (Free / Available).

calendarId.description: Optional. The calendar ID to create the event on. The default is the user's primary calendar.

colorId.description: Optional. The color of the event. This is an ID referring to an entry in the calendar's color palette. Event color ID (string `1`-`11`): * 1: Lavender * 2: Sage * 3: Grape * 4: Flamingo * 5: Banana * 6: Tangerine * 7: Peacock * 8: Graphite * 9: Blueberry * 10: Basil * 11: Tomato.

description.description: Optional. Description of the event. Can contain HTML.

endTime.description: Required. The end time of the event. Must be a full ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., '2026-04-30T10:00:00Z').

eventType.description: Optional. Type of the event. Optional. Possible values for creation are: * `DEFAULT` - Default event type. * `OUT_OF_OFFICE` - Out of office event type. * `FOCUS_TIME` - Focus time event type. * `WORKING_LOCATION` - Working location event type. * `BIRTHDAY` - Birthday event type.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Will be treated as DEFAULT.
A regular event. Default value.
An out-of-office event.
A focus-time event.
A working location event.
A special all-day event with an annual recurrence.
An event from Gmail. This type of event cannot be created.

googleMeetUrl.description: Optional. Allows attaching an existing Google Meet URL or meeting ID to the event.

guestPermissions.description: Optional. Guest permissions settings for this event. Optional.

location.description: Optional. Geographic location of the event as free-form text.

notificationLevel.description: Optional. Which email notification should be sent for this event update. Possible values are: * `NONE` - No email notifications are sent. * `EXTERNAL_ONLY` - Only external (non-Calendar) attendees receive email notifications (default). * `ALL` - All event attendees receive email notifications.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. Will be treated as EXTERNAL_ONLY.
No email notifications are sent.
Only external (non-Calendar) attendees receive email notifications.
All event attendees receive email notifications.

overrideReminders.description: Optional. Reminders defined for this event, overriding the default reminders for the calendar.

recurrenceData.description: Optional. The recurrence data of the event as RRULE, RDATE, or EXDATE strings per RFC 5545.

startTime.description: Required. The start time of the event formatted as per ISO 8601.

timeZone.description: Optional. Time zone of the event formatted as a strictly case-sensitive IANA Time Zone Database name (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles').

visibility.description: Optional. Visibility of the event. Possible values are: * `default` * `public` * `private`.

workingLocationProperties.description: Optional. Properties for working location events. Optional.

calendar.name: update_event
calendar.description: Updates a calendar event. Use this tool for queries like: * Update the event 'Meeting with Jane' to be one hour later. * Add john.doe@google.com to the meeting tomorrow. Example: update_event( eventId='event123', summary='Meeting with Jane and John' ) # Updates the summary of event with id 'event123' on the primary calendar.

Attachment.description: A file attachment for an event.

fileUrl.description: Required. URL link to the attachment.

additionalGuests.description: Optional. Number of additional guests. Optional. The default is 0.

comment.description: Output only. The attendee's response comment. Optional. Read-only.

email.description: Required. The attendee's email address. This field must be present when adding an attendee. It must be a valid email address as per RFC5322.

id.description: Output only. The attendee's Profile ID. Optional. Read-only.

optionalAttendee.description: Optional. Whether this is an optional attendee. Optional. The default is False.

organizer.description: Output only. Whether the attendee is the organizer of the event. Read-only. The default is False.

resource.description: Optional. Whether the attendee is a resource. Can only be set when the attendee is added to the event for the first time. Subsequent modifications are ignored. Optional. The default is False.

responseStatus.description: Optional. The attendee's response status. Possible values are: * `needsAction` - The attendee has not responded to the invitation (recommended for new events). * `declined` - The attendee has declined the invitation. * `tentative` - The attendee has tentatively accepted the invitation. * `accepted` - The attendee has accepted the invitation.

self.description: Output only. Whether this entry represents the calendar on which this copy of the event appears. Read-only. The default is False.

guestsCanInviteOthers.description: Optional. Whether attendees other than the organizer can invite others to the event. Optional.

guestsCanModify.description: Optional. Whether attendees other than the organizer can modify the event. Optional.

guestsCanSeeGuests.description: Optional. Whether attendees other than the organizer can see who the event's attendees are. Optional.

method.description: Required. How the reminder is delivered to the user. Possible values are: * `email` - Reminders are sent via email. * `popup` - Reminders are sent via a UI popup.

minutes.description: Required. Number of minutes in advance that the reminder should be delivered.

addGoogleMeetUrl.description: Optional. Allows to create or update a Google Meet url for the event.

addedAttachments.description: Optional. File attachments to add to the event.

addedAttendees.description: Optional. The additional attendees of the event.

allDay.description: Optional. Whether the event is an all-day event. Will not be updated if not set.

availability.description: Optional. Whether the event blocks time on the calendar. Optional.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. Will be treated as BUSY.
The event blocks time on the calendar.
The event does not block time on the calendar (Free / Available).

calendarId.description: Optional. The calendar ID of the event to update. The default is the user's primary calendar.

colorId.description: Optional. New color ID of the event. Will not be updated if not set. Event color ID (string `1`-`11`): * 1: Lavender * 2: Sage * 3: Grape * 4: Flamingo * 5: Banana * 6: Tangerine * 7: Peacock * 8: Graphite * 9: Blueberry * 10: Basil * 11: Tomato.

description.description: Optional. The new description of the event. Will not be updated if not set.

endTime.description: Optional. The new end time of the event formatted as per ISO 8601. Will not be updated if not set.

eventId.description: Required. The ID of the event to update.

googleMeetUrl.description: Optional. Allows attaching an existing Google Meet URL or meeting ID to the event.

guestPermissions.description: Optional. Guest permissions settings for this event. Optional.

location.description: Optional. The new location of the event. Will not be updated if not set.

notificationLevel.description: Optional. Which email notification should be sent for this event update. Possible values are: * `NONE` * `EXTERNAL_ONLY` * `ALL`.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. Will be treated as EXTERNAL_ONLY.
No email notifications are sent.
Only external (non-Calendar) attendees receive email notifications.
All event attendees receive email notifications.

overrideReminders.description: Optional. Reminders defined for this event, overriding any existing reminders and the default reminders for the calendar.

removedAttachmentFileUrls.description: Optional. File attachments to remove from the event.

removedAttendeeEmails.description: Optional. The attendees of the event to remove, as email addresses.

startTime.description: Optional. The new start time of the event formatted as per ISO 8601. Will not be updated if not set.

summary.description: Optional. The new title of the event. Will not be updated if not set.

timeZone.description: Optional. Time zone of the event formatted as a strictly case-sensitive IANA Time Zone Database name (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles').

visibility.description: Optional. New visibility of the event. Possible values are: * `default` * `public` * `private`.

calendar.name: list_events
calendar.description: Lists calendar events in a given calendar satisfying the given conditions. Key Features: * Any Calendar ID. * Time range filtering. * Retrieves ALL events matching the time constraints. Use this tool for queries like: * What's on my calendar tomorrow? * What are my meetings next week? * Do I have any conflicts this afternoon?

calendarId.description: Optional. The calendar ID to list events from. The default is the user's primary calendar.

endTime.description: Optional. Upper bound (exclusive) for the time window to search. Defaults to the end of time. Must be an ISO 8601 timestamp strictly greater than start_time.

eventType.description: Optional. The event types to return.

fullText.description: Optional. Free-form search query to search across title, description, location and attendees. The search is case-insensitive and supports substring matching.

orderBy.description: Optional. The order in which events should be returned. Possible values are: * `default` * `startTime` * `startTimeDesc` * `lastModified`.

pageSize.description: Optional. Maximum number of events returned on one result page. By default the value is 100 events. It is recommended to set pageSize=10 for most queries to save context.

pageToken.description: Optional. A token retrieved from a previous response's nextPageToken field.

startTime.description: Optional. Lower bound (exclusive) for the time window to search. Defaults to the current time if neither time bound is provided.

timeZone.description: Optional. Time zone used in the response and to resolve timezone-less dates in the request.

calendar.name: get_event
calendar.description: Returns a single event from a given calendar. Use this tool for queries like: * Get details for the team meeting. * Show me the event with id event123 on my calendar.

calendarId.description: Optional. The calendar ID to get the event from. The default is the user's primary calendar.

eventId.description: Required. The ID of the event to get.

calendar.name: delete_event
calendar.description: Deletes a calendar event. Use this tool for queries like: * Delete the event with id event123 on my calendar. To cancel or decline an event, use the respond_to_event tool instead.

calendarId.description: Optional. The calendar ID of the event to delete. The default is the user's primary calendar.

eventId.description: Required. The ID of the event to delete.

notificationLevel.description: Optional. Which email notification should be sent for this event update. Possible values are: * `NONE` * `EXTERNAL_ONLY` * `ALL`.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. Will be treated as EXTERNAL_ONLY.
No email notifications are sent.
Only external (non-Calendar) attendees receive email notifications.
All event attendees receive email notifications.

gmail.name: create_draft
gmail.description: Creates a new draft email in the authenticated user's Gmail account. This tool takes recipient addresses, a subject, and body content as inputs. It returns the ID of the created Gmail draft. If the draft is created as a reply to an existing message, the ID of the original message should be passed to the tool in the replyToMessageId field. Creating drafts with attachments is not supported yet.

Attachment.description: Represents an attachment to be included in an email.

content.description: Required. The base64-encoded content of the attachment.

filename.description: Optional. The name of the file to be attached, e.g. "invoice.pdf". For inline attachments, this is used for Content-ID generation. For regular attachments, filename is used to specify the filename to email clients. If not provided, the attachment may be received with no name.

id.description: Optional. Output only. When present, contains the ID of an external attachment that can be retrieved in a separate `GetMessageAttachment` request.

inline.description: Optional. If true, this attachment is handled as inline. An inline attachment is a content that is intended to be displayed within the body of an HTML email, as opposed to being listed as a separate file for download. If false or absent, defaults to false, and it's treated as a regular attachment.

mimeType.description: Optional. The field representing a content or media type must use IANA MIME type. If not provided, defaults to "application/octet-stream".

attachments.description: Optional. The attachments to include in the email. The combined size of attachments in the message cannot exceed 25MB.

bcc.description: Optional. The blind carbon copy recipients of the email draft. Each string MUST be a valid plain email address (e.g., "user@example.com").

body.description: Optional. The main body content of the email draft. If html_body is also provided, this field is treated as the plain-text alternative.

cc.description: Optional. The carbon copy recipients of the email draft. Each string MUST be a valid plain email address (e.g., "user@example.com").

htmlBody.description: The HTML content of the email draft. If provided, this will be used as the rich-text version of the email.

replyToMessageId.description: Optional. The ID of the message to reply to. If provided, this will be used as the reply-to message ID for the email draft.

subject.description: Optional. The subject line of the email. Defaults to empty if not provided.

to.description: Required. The primary recipients of the email draft. Each string MUST be a valid plain email address (e.g., "user@example.com").

gmail.name: create_label
gmail.description: Creates a new label in the authenticated user's Gmail account. Supports creating nested labels (sub-labels) using a forward slash (e.g., 'Projects/Alpha/Sprint-1'). By default, parent labels will be automatically created if they do not exist.

backgroundColor.description: The background color of the label, represented as a hex string (e.g., "#ffffff"). Only the following predefined set of color values are allowed: #000000, #434343, #666666, #999999, #cccccc, #efefef, #f3f3f3, #ffffff, #fb4c2f, #ffad47, #fad165, #16a766, #43d692, #4a86e8, #a479e2, #f691b3, #f6c5be, #ffe6c7, #fef1d1, #b9e4d0, #c6f3de, #c9daf8, #e4d7f5, #fcdee8, #efa093, #ffd6a2, #fce8b3, #89d3b2, #a0eac9, #a4c2f4, #d0bcf1, #fbc8d9, #e66550, #ffbc6b, #fcda83, #44b984, #68dfa9, #6d9eeb, #b694e8, #f7a7c0, #cc3a21, #eaa041, #f2c960, #149e60, #3dc789, #3c78d8, #8e63ce, #e07798, #ac2b16, #cf8933, #d5ae49, #0b804b, #2a9c68, #285bac, #653e9b, #b65775, #822111, #a46a21, #aa8831, #076239, #1a764d, #1c4587, #41236d, #83334c, #464646, #e7e7e7, #0d3472, #b6cff5, #0d3b44, #98d7e4, #3d188e, #e3d7ff, #711a36, #fbd3e0, #8a1c0a, #f2b2a8, #7a2e0b, #ffc8af, #7a4706, #ffdeb5, #594c05, #fbe983, #684e07, #fdedc1, #0b4f30, #b3efd3, #04502e, #a2dcc1, #c2c2c2, #4986e7, #2da2bb, #b99aff, #994a64, #f691b2, #ff7537, #ffad46, #662e37, #ebdbde, #cca6ac, #094228, #42d692, #16a765

autoCreateParentLabels.description: Optional. Whether to automatically create parent labels for nested labels (separated by '/'). Defaults to true.

color.description: Optional. The color of the label.

displayName.description: Required. The display name of the label to create.

gmail.name: search_threads
gmail.description: Lists email threads from the authenticated user's Gmail account. This tool can filter threads based on a query string and supports pagination. It returns a list of threads, including their IDs and related messages. Note that the full message bodies are not returned by this tool; use the 'get_thread' tool with a thread ID to fetch the full message body if needed.

includeTrash.description: Optional. Include drafts from TRASH in the results. Defaults to false.

pageSize.description: Optional. The maximum number of threads to return. If unspecified, defaults to 20. The maximum allowed value is 50.

pageToken.description: Optional. Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list. Leave empty to fetch the first page.

query.description: Optional. A query string to filter the threads. Natural language queries must be pre-converted into Gmail syntax queries. Supported Operators by Category: Sender & Recipient: from:, to:, cc:, bcc:, deliveredto:, list:. Time & Date: after:YYYY/MM/DD, before:YYYY/MM/DD, older_than:, newer_than:. Content: subject:, has:, filename:, AROUND. Labels & Categories: label:, category:, in:. Status: is:. Size: size:, larger:, smaller:. Logic & Grouping: AND, OR, { }, - (minus), ( ).

gmail.name: list_labels
gmail.description: Lists all user-defined labels available in the authenticated user's Gmail account. Use this tool to discover the `id` of a user label before calling `label_thread`, `unlabel_thread`, `label_message`, or `unlabel_message`. System labels are not returned by this tool but can be used with their well-known IDs: 'INBOX', 'TRASH', 'SPAM', 'STARRED', 'UNREAD', 'IMPORTANT', 'CHAT', 'DRAFT', 'SENT'.

pageSize.description: Optional. The maximum number of labels to return.

pageToken.description: Optional. Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list.

drive.name: search_files
drive.description: Search for Drive files using a structured query (syntax: `query_term operator values`). Combine clauses with `and`, `or`, `not`, and parentheses. String values must be single-quoted; escape embedded quotes as \'. Query terms & operators: - `title` (ops: contains, =, !=) - `fullText` (ops: contains) - `mimeType` (ops: contains, =, !=) - `modifiedTime`, `viewedByMeTime`, `createdTime` (ops: <=, <, =, !=, >, >=). Use RFC 3339 UTC. - `parentId` (ops: =, !=). Use 'root' for the user's "My Drive". - `owner` (ops: =, !=). Use 'me' for the requesting user. - `sharedWithMe` (ops: =, !=). Values: true or false.

excludeContentSnippets.description: If true, the content snippet will be excluded from the response.

pageSize.description: The maximum number of files to return in each page.

pageToken.description: The page token to use for pagination.

drive.name: create_file
drive.description: Call this tool to create or upload a File to Google Drive. If uploading content, prefer "text_content" for text content. For non-UTF8 contents, use the "base64_content" field and base64 encode the data. Returns a single File object upon successful creation. Folders can be created by setting the mime type to `application/vnd.google-apps.folder`.

base64Content.description: Optional. The base64 encoded content to upload. It's an error to set this and text_content.

content.description: The content of the file encoded as base64. DEPRECATED. Use base64_content or text_content instead.

contentMimeType.description: The mime type of the content being uploaded. Required when any type of content is provided.

disableConversionToGoogleType.description: Set to true to retain the passed in content mime type and not convert to a Google type.

mimeType.description: DEPRECATED. DO NOT USE!! Set content_mime_type instead.

parentId.description: The parent id of the file.

textContent.description: Optional. The (UTF-8) text content to upload. It's an error to set this and base64_content.

drive.name: copy_file
drive.description: Call this tool to copy an existing File in Google Drive. The tool allows specifying a new title and a parent folder for the copy. If the title is not specified, the copy title will be 'Copy of {original title}'. If the parent folder is not specified, the copy will be created in the same folder as the original file.

fileId.description: Required. The ID of the file to copy.

parentId.description: The parent id of the newly created file. If empty, the file will be created with the same parent as the original file.

title.description: The title of the newly created file. If empty, the title will be 'Copy of [original file title]'.
